title: Refine in-game basket expand/collapse UX across scene and HUD clicks
goal: Replace the in-game basket expand/collapse button flow with basket-surface click opening and context-aware auto-collapse so non-transfer scene clicks and top-left HUD clicks close the current basket while valid ingredient transfer clicks keep it open.
constraints: Game runtime only; do not change admin preview/editor behavior in this task
Preserve CSS 3D transform order and preserve-3d chain
Keep scene-level rect hit-testing; do not switch to pointer-events-based 3D hit detection
Include SelectionDisplay and Handbar clicks as outside-click collapse triggers
Do not globalize the whole interaction state just to support HUD collapse; use a narrow collapse signal/callback path
Do not regress burner or sink scene-level input ownership
Do not revert pre-existing dirty changes in GameKitchenView
acceptance: Clicking a basket surface opens it without relying on a visible expand/collapse button
Clicking empty scene space closes any expanded basket
Clicking another storage toggle target such as a drawer or another basket closes the current basket and still performs the target toggle in the same click
With an ingredient selected from a basket, clicking a valid transfer destination such as a wok hologram or placed bowl keeps the basket open while the transfer proceeds
Clicking SelectionDisplay closes any expanded basket and preserves its current deselect behavior
Clicking Handbar blank area closes any expanded basket and preserves current move-to-hand behavior when applicable
Clicking a Handbar ingredient chip closes any expanded basket and preserves current chip select/deselect behavior
Fridge-internal baskets follow the same keep-open vs auto-close rules without breaking fridge door open filtering
Burner and sink scene-level interactions remain unchanged
